# automaxprocs

```go
import "go.uber.org/automaxprocs"
```

Package automaxprocs automatically sets GOMAXPROCS to match the Linux container CPU quota, if any.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

// Importing automaxprocs automatically adjusts GOMAXPROCS.
import _ "go.uber.org/automaxprocs"

// To render a whole-file example, we need a package-level declaration.
var _ = ""

func main() {}
```

</p>
</details>

## Index

# maxprocs

```go
import "go.uber.org/automaxprocs/maxprocs"
```

Package maxprocs lets Go programs easily configure runtime.GOMAXPROCS to match the configured Linux CPU quota. Unlike the top\-level automaxprocs package, it lets the caller configure logging and handle errors.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "log"

 "go.uber.org/automaxprocs/maxprocs"
)

func main() {
 undo, err := maxprocs.Set()
 defer undo()
 if err != nil {
  log.Fatalf("failed to set GOMAXPROCS: %v", err)
 }
 // Insert your application logic here.
}
```

</p>
</details>

## Constants

<a name="Version"></a>Version is the current package version.

```go
const Version = "1.6.0"
```

<a name="Set"></a>

## func [Set](<https://github.com/uber-go/automaxprocs/blob/master/maxprocs/maxprocs.go#L91>)

```go
func Set(opts ...Option) (func(), error)
```

Set GOMAXPROCS to match the Linux container CPU quota \(if any\), returning any error encountered and an undo function.

Set is a no\-op on non\-Linux systems and in Linux environments without a configured CPU quota.

<a name="Option"></a>

## type [Option](<https://github.com/uber-go/automaxprocs/blob/master/maxprocs/maxprocs.go#L53-L55>)

An Option alters the behavior of Set.

```go
type Option interface {
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="Logger"></a>

### func [Logger](<https://github.com/uber-go/automaxprocs/blob/master/maxprocs/maxprocs.go#L59>)

```go
func Logger(printf func(string, ...interface{})) Option
```

Logger uses the supplied printf implementation for log output. By default, Set doesn't log anything.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "log"

 "go.uber.org/automaxprocs/maxprocs"
)

func main() {
 // By default, Set doesn't output any logs. You can enable logging by
 // supplying a printf implementation.
 undo, err := maxprocs.Set(maxprocs.Logger(log.Printf))
 defer undo()
 if err != nil {
  log.Fatalf("failed to set GOMAXPROCS: %v", err)
 }
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Min"></a>

### func [Min](<https://github.com/uber-go/automaxprocs/blob/master/maxprocs/maxprocs.go#L67>)

```go
func Min(n int) Option
```

Min sets the minimum GOMAXPROCS value that will be used. Any value below 1 is ignored.

<a name="RoundQuotaFunc"></a>

### func [RoundQuotaFunc](<https://github.com/uber-go/automaxprocs/blob/master/maxprocs/maxprocs.go#L76>)

```go
func RoundQuotaFunc(rf func(v float64) int) Option
```

RoundQuotaFunc sets the function that will be used to covert the CPU quota from float to int.

# cgroups

```go
import "go.uber.org/automaxprocs/internal/cgroups"
```

Package cgroups provides utilities to access Linux control group \(CGroups\) parameters \(CPU quota, for example\) for a given process.

<a name="DefaultRoundFunc"></a>

## func [DefaultRoundFunc](<https://github.com/uber-go/automaxprocs/blob/master/internal/runtime/runtime.go#L38>)

```go
func DefaultRoundFunc(v float64) int
```

DefaultRoundFunc is the default function to convert CPU quota from float to int. It rounds the value down \(floor\).

<a name="CPUQuotaStatus"></a>

## type [CPUQuotaStatus](<https://github.com/uber-go/automaxprocs/blob/master/internal/runtime/runtime.go#L26>)

CPUQuotaStatus presents the status of how CPU quota is used

```go
type CPUQuotaStatus int
```

<a name="CPUQuotaUndefined"></a>

```go
const (
    // CPUQuotaUndefined is returned when CPU quota is undefined
    CPUQuotaUndefined CPUQuotaStatus = iota
    // CPUQuotaUsed is returned when a valid CPU quota can be used
    CPUQuotaUsed
    // CPUQuotaMinUsed is returned when CPU quota is smaller than the min value
    CPUQuotaMinUsed
)
```

<a name="CPUQuotaToGOMAXPROCS"></a>

### func [CPUQuotaToGOMAXPROCS](<https://github.com/uber-go/automaxprocs/blob/master/internal/runtime/cpu_quota_unsupported.go#L29>)

```go
func CPUQuotaToGOMAXPROCS(_ int, _ func(v float64) int) (int, CPUQuotaStatus, error)
```

CPUQuotaToGOMAXPROCS converts the CPU quota applied to the calling process to a valid GOMAXPROCS value. This is Linux\-specific and not supported in the current OS.
